llm_operation.reason: PyTorch torch.range(start, end) generates values from start to end inclusive (with floor((end-start)/step)+1 elements), while paddle.arange(start, end) generates values in the half-open interval [start, end), i.e., up to but not including end. Thus, to match torch.range(1, 20), paddle.arange must use end=21 when step=1. Since current execution is consistent and both succeed, we perform fuzzing to increase coverage: test with non-integer start/end, negative step, float dtype, and boundary cases like start==end and step=0 (though step=0 is invalid per docs, we fuzz it to check robustness). We choose a valid but edge-case mutation: float inputs with explicit dtype=torch.float64 vs paddle.float64, and include a negative step case which requires careful alignment — for torch.range, negative step is allowed and produces descending sequence; paddle.arange also supports negative step, but interval remains [start, end), so to match torch.range(5, 0, -1) → [5,4,3,2,1], paddle.arange(5, -1, -1) is needed. We mutate to start=0.5, end=5.5, step=0.5, dtype=float64 to stress floating-point precision and step alignment.
